<!-- Short, practical guidance for AI coding agents working in this repo. Keep this file <= ~50 lines. -->
# Copilot / AI agent instructions — Robot2026

Purpose
- Help AI agents be productive quickly: show entry points, conventions, integration points, and concrete examples to edit safely.

Quick orientation
- Entrypoints: `robot.py` (runtime entry) and `robotcontainer.py` (wiring of subsystems, commands, and operator bindings).
- Subsystems live in `subsystems/` (e.g. `fuel_subsystem.py`, `elevator_subsystem.py`, `command_swerve_drivetrain.py`).
- Central constants: `constants.py`. Do NOT manually edit files in `generated/` (e.g. `generated/tuner_constants.py`) — they are autogenerated and will be overwritten.

Architecture notes (what to read first)
- Read `robotcontainer.py` to understand how subsystems and commands are constructed and how operator bindings map to commands.
- Inspect a subsystem file (start with `subsystems/fuel_subsystem.py`) to see common patterns for hardware access, periodic updates, and command endpoints.
- `deploy/pathplanner/` contains path planner assets (paths, autos); Path files are static assets loaded at runtime.

Conventions and patterns (concrete)
- Hardware & tuning: default IDs and constants are in `constants.py`; runtime-tuned overrides may appear in `generated/tuner_constants.py`.
- Subsystems implement WPILib-style methods and expose command-friendly methods (e.g., `setXX`, `stop`, `periodic`). Follow naming in existing subsystems.
- Commands and higher-level behaviors are wired in `robotcontainer.py` or near the subsystem where simple commands exist (see `command_swerve_drivetrain.py`).

Dev workflows & tests (what we know)
- There is a pyproject.toml at repo root describing Python deps and project metadata. Use it to infer the test/runtime environment.
- Tests: `tests/pyfrc_test.py` is present — run unit/pyfrc tests via the project's test runner (confirm whether this repo uses `pytest` or `pyfrc` CLI in CI). If unsure, ask maintainer before changing test config.

Integration points & files to avoid editing
- `generated/` — autogenerated constants; changes here will be lost. If you need permanent changes, update the generator/source tool or `constants.py`.
- `deploy/pathplanner/` — assets (paths, autos). Editing is allowed but be careful: path format matters for the path-loading code.

Examples to copy patterns from
- To wire a new subsystem: follow `robotcontainer.py` and mimic how existing subsystems are instantiated and how command bindings are added.
- To add a configurable constant: add to `constants.py` and reference from subsystems; if a value is intended to be tuned at runtime, note the presence of `generated/tuner_constants.py`.

When in doubt
- Prefer minimal, reversible changes in new PRs. Run tests (ask for exact test command if not obvious). Mention any modification to `generated/` or `deploy/` explicitly in PR body.

Missing items — please provide
- CI test command (how to run unit and integration tests locally and in CI).
- Whether `generated/` files are produced by a local tool and how to regenerate them.
- Any project-specific linters or formatting rules not visible in repo files.

If anything here is inaccurate or you want more detail, tell me which area to expand (tests, CI, generator tooling, or example-driven edits).
